sidebar.title: Goiás Verde Project
sidebar.logo_fallback: CEAGRE logo found but it could not be rendered in this environment.
login.title: Goiás Verde Project
login.caption: Sign in to access the Streamlit application.
login.error_credentials_missing: Supabase authentication is enabled but credentials are not configured.
login.info_configure_secrets: Add credentials to `.streamlit/secrets.toml` following the example in `.streamlit/secrets.toml.example`.
login.signup_caption: Create your account. A confirmation email will be sent after sign-up.
login.signup_success: Account created. Check your inbox to confirm the email before signing in.
auth.error.fill_credentials: Fill in email and password before continuing.
auth.error.password_too_short: Password must be at least 6 characters long.
auth.error.invalid_credentials: Wrong email or password. Please check and try again.
auth.error.email_not_confirmed: Email not confirmed yet. Check your inbox.
auth.error.rate_limit: Too many sign-in attempts. Please wait a few minutes and try again.
auth.error.signup_rate_limit: Too many attempts. Please wait a few minutes and try again.
auth.error.weak_password: Weak password. Use at least 6 characters with letters and numbers.
auth.error.no_session: Supabase did not return an active session. Check your credentials and confirm the email.
auth.error.missing_credentials_env: Auth is enabled but `supabase.url` and `supabase.publishable_key` are not configured.
upload.title: File Upload
upload.uploader_label: Upload your CSV, TXT/TSV or Excel
upload.error_inspect: Could not inspect the uploaded file: {error}
upload.success_loaded: Dataset loaded successfully: {rows} rows x {cols} columns
upload.schema.title: Expected schema validation
upload.schema.caption: The dataset is checked against the plant-physiology reference schema (IRGA, chlorophyll meter, ceptometer). Missing columns do not block the app — they simply disable specific features.
upload.schema.required_missing: Missing required columns: {cols}. Some analyses will be unavailable.
upload.schema.required_empty: Required columns are present but 100% empty: {cols}. The pipeline may drop every row — check the spreadsheet.
upload.schema.recommended_empty: Recommended columns are present but 100% empty: {cols}.
upload.schema.col.label: Expected column
pipeline.title: Pipeline & Processing
pipeline.warn_no_data: Upload a file in the Upload tab before processing.
pipeline.drop.help: Selected columns are removed at the start of the pipeline.
pipeline.threshold.caption: Useful when the flux is close to zero and R² is no longer informative: filter by coefficient of variation (CV) or any other user-defined quality variable.
pipeline.threshold.help: Pick one or more numeric columns (e.g. CV for CO₂, CH₄, N₂O). Rows with NaN in those columns are removed.
pipeline.warn_threshold_no_cols: Threshold filter skipped: none of the selected columns exist in the dataset.
pipeline.seasonal_q.caption: For each selected column, computes Q10 and Q90 within each level of the seasonal column and drops values outside [Q10 − k·(Q90−Q10), Q90 + k·(Q90−Q10)]. NaNs are preserved.
pipeline.warn_seasonal_q_missing: Q10-Q90 cleaning skipped: seasonal column '{col}' not found.
pipeline.warn_seasonal_q_no_cols: Q10-Q90 cleaning skipped: no valid column selected.
pipeline.rep.caption: Aggregation runs after the filters and computes the mean/median of remaining replicates.
pipeline.rep.tip: Tip: avoid using Latitude/Longitude as group key if they vary between REP_1/REP_2/REP_3.
pipeline.warn_diag_missing: Diagnostic filter skipped: column '{col}' not found (it may have been removed).
pipeline.warn_outlier_group: Outliers per group switched to global: column '{col}' was removed.
pipeline.warn_outlier_no_cols: Outlier filter skipped: all selected columns were removed.
pipeline.warn_rep_missing: Replicate aggregation skipped: REP column '{col}' was removed.
pipeline.warn_rep_no_keys: Replicate aggregation has no valid keys after column removal.
pipeline.warn_heavy_discard: The pipeline removed {pct}% of rows ({before} → {after}). Check whether essential columns (Cultura, Uso atual, Época) are filled in the spreadsheet.
pipeline.warn_step_discard: Step "{step}" removed {pct}% of rows ({before} → {after}). May indicate large-scale missing data — investigate before proceeding.
pipeline.report_empty: Run the pipeline to generate the report.
eda.title: EDA
eda.warn_no_data: Upload a file in the Upload tab.
eda.warn_no_numeric: Dataset has no numeric columns for EDA.
eda.summary.title: Statistical summary of numeric variables
eda.quality.title: Data quality
eda.quality.confounding_caption: Pairs of categorical columns that partition rows the same way. When two columns are redundant (e.g. Fazenda ⟷ Cultura), any effect attributed to one is statistically indistinguishable from the other — read Moran's I, Kruskal-Wallis and group comparisons cautiously.
eda.quality.confounding_none: No categorical pairs showed confounding (Cramér's V < 0.95 in both directions).
eda.quality.confounding_warning: There are fully redundant pairs (one column is just a relabeling of the other). Do not use both as independent factors in models or comparisons.
eda.bivariate.title: Univariate distributions
eda.boxplot.title: Boxplots by group
eda.boxplot.no_cat: No categorical columns available for this block.
eda.scatter.title: Scatter matrix of variables
eda.scatter.sample: Maximum sample size for the plot
eda.scatter.info_min: Select at least 2 variables to build the scatter matrix.
eda.scatter.warn_max: Select at most 6 variables to keep readability.
eda.scatter.caption_sample: Pairplot using a sample of {n} rows for performance.
eda.corr.title: Correlation matrix
eda.corr.info_min: Select at least 2 variables to compute correlation.
eda.normality.title: Normality tests
eda.normality.caption: Shapiro-Wilk, Anderson-Darling and D'Agostino-Pearson applied to each variable; the normal_at_alpha column is True when none of the three tests reject the normality hypothesis.
eda.normality.large_n_hint: For n above a few hundred the three tests reject normality easily even for tiny deviations. Use the Q-Q plot below to judge the magnitude of the deviation.
eda.vif.title: Multicollinearity — VIF
eda.vif.caption: Variance Inflation Factor for each variable; values ≥ 5 indicate moderate collinearity and ≥ 10 severe collinearity.
eda.vif.derived_note: Mathematically derived variables (Ci/Ca, A/Ci, EUA=A/E, ETR from YII) inflate VIF by construction — this does not signal poor data quality. Interpret high VIF only across original, independent variables.
eda.vif.missing_statsmodels: statsmodels is not installed; run `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
eda.inference.title: Kruskal-Wallis test between groups
eda.inference.caption: Compares numeric distributions across levels of a categorical column (non-parametric).
eda.inference.missing_scipy: scipy is not installed; run `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
eda.inference.no_cat: No categorical columns available for grouping.
eda.inference.min_per_group_help: Groups with fewer samples are dropped before the test. Kruskal-Wallis has low power and inaccurate p-values with very small groups (n < 5).
eda.inference.dropped_caption: Levels with n < {n} were dropped before the test — see the dropped_levels column.
eda.hotspots.title: Hotspots ranking
eda.hotspots.caption: Top-N sampling units by median/mean of a flux variable, optionally faceted by season or crop.
eda.hotspots.no_cat: No categorical columns available for grouping.
eda.outliers.title: Multi-method outlier detection
eda.outliers.caption: Applies Z-score, IQR, Isolation Forest, LOF and Elliptic Envelope to the selected variables. The consensus_outlier column flags rows flagged by at least 3 methods.
eda.outliers.assumptions: Assumptions: Z-score assumes near-normal distribution (non-robust); IQR is robust; Isolation Forest and LOF are non-parametric; Elliptic Envelope assumes multivariate normality and is unreliable for bimodal or strongly skewed data. Interpret the consensus with this in mind.
eda.outliers.missing_sklearn: scikit-learn not available for the multivariate methods.
eda.outliers.too_few: Too few data points (<20 rows) for robust detection.
eda.spatial.title: Spatial analysis
eda.temporal.title: Time series of fluxes
eda.temporal.no_date: Date column not found (Data, Date or DATE).
eda.composition.title: Categorical composition of the dataset
eda.composition.no_cat: No categorical columns available for this block.
regression.title: Regression
regression.warn_no_data: Upload a file in the Upload tab.
regression.warn_min_numeric: At least 2 numeric columns are required for regression.
regression.preset.swc_co2: Soil water content x CO₂ flux
regression.preset_caption_sample: Sample of 3000 rows used in the preset for performance.
regression.q10.caption: Exponential fit Flux = R₀ · Q₁₀^((T - {tref})/10). Estimates: R₀ = {r0:.4f}, Q₁₀ = {q10:.3f} (R² = {r2:.3f}, n = {n}).
regression.q10.warn_low: Too few positive samples to fit Q₁₀.
regression.caption_sample: Sample of {n} rows used for performance.
modeling.title: Modeling
modeling.warn_no_data: Upload a file in the Upload tab.
modeling.warn_min_numeric: Numeric columns required for modeling are not enough.
modeling.warn_min_model: Select at least one model to compare.
modeling.cv_strategy_help: GroupKFold keeps every replicate from the same site (e.g. Fazenda + Ponto) in the same fold, preventing the model from seeing rows correlated with the test set during training. Recommended whenever pseudoreplication is present.
modeling.warn_no_group_col: No suitable categorical column found for grouping. Falling back to random KFold.
modeling.warn_groups_lt_folds: Only {n_groups} groups available; reducing folds from {folds} to {new_folds}.
modeling.warn_too_few_rows: Too few rows after filters to train a model (<30 rows).
modeling.predicted_caption: Dashed line: ideal 1:1. Points close to the line indicate a better fit.
spatial.title: Spatial Analysis
spatial.warn_no_data: Upload a file in the Upload tab.
spatial.kriging.title: Empirical variogram + Ordinary kriging
spatial.kriging.caption: Computes the empirical variogram in up to 12 distance bins, fits a spherical model and estimates the surface via ordinary kriging. Computationally intensive — enable kriging only after inspecting the variogram.
spatial.kriging.few_lags: Could not compute enough lags; adjust the max-fraction slider.
spatial.kriging.units_caption: Variogram and kriging are computed in UTM metres ({crs}). Before v1.1 distance was in lat/lon degrees — old range values are not directly comparable.
spatial.gistar.d_star_caption: Distance-band threshold d*: {d_star} m (in {crs}). Computed as the largest k-th neighbour distance × 1.001.
spatial.kriging.toggle_to_run: Tick the checkbox to generate the kriged surface (computationally expensive).
spatial.gistar.title: Getis-Ord Gi* hotspots
spatial.gistar.caption: Detects local concentrations of high (Hotspot) or low (Coldspot) values using Gi* based on k nearest neighbours and a permutation p-value.
spatial.gistar.missing_deps: esda not installed; run `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
spatial.utmgrid.title: Regular UTM grid aggregation
spatial.utmgrid.caption: Reprojects points to an appropriate metric UTM system and aggregates them in cells of configurable size (median/mean and count).
spatial.utmgrid.missing_deps: geopandas/shapely not installed; run `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
spatial.idw.title: Inverse Distance Weighting (IDW) interpolation
spatial.idw.caption: Generates smoothed surfaces from sparse sampling points; useful when sample size is too small for kriging.
spatial.moran.title: Spatial autocorrelation — Moran's I and LISA
spatial.moran.caption: Detects global spatial dependence (Moran's I) and local clusters HH/LL/HL/LH (LISA).
spatial.moran.missing_deps: libpysal/esda not installed; run `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
spatial.basemap.title: Rio Verde basemap with sampling points
spatial.basemap.caption: Overlays sampling points on the municipality boundary (geobr); requires internet on first run.
spatial.basemap.unavailable: Could not load the municipality boundary (geobr unavailable or offline).
spatial.basemap.title_dynamic: Sampling points of {var} over Rio Verde, GO
timeseries.title: Time Series
timeseries.warn_no_data: Upload a file in the Upload tab.
timeseries.no_date: Date column not found (Data, Date, Date_Time, etc.).
timeseries.agg.title: Daily aggregated time series
timeseries.agg.caption: Aggregates fluxes by day using mean or median and plots the series side by side.
timeseries.stl.title: STL decomposition (trend + seasonal + residual)
timeseries.stl.caption: Robust decomposition of a univariate time series (Cleveland et al., 1990).
timeseries.stl.missing_deps: statsmodels not installed; run `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
timeseries.stl.too_few: Series too short ({n} points), needs at least {need}.
timeseries.stl.too_few_dates: Only {n} dates have actual measurements (minimum {need}). STL decomposition needs enough observed points — sparse field campaigns do not meet the requirement.
timeseries.stl.heavy_interpolation: Only {observed} of {total} days have actual measurements ({pct}%). Most of the series is interpolated — trend/seasonal strength reflects the interpolation, not the observed signal.
comparative.title: Two-group comparison
comparative.warn_no_data: Upload a file in the Upload tab.
comparative.no_cat: Dataset has no categorical columns to use as a comparison dimension.
comparative.config.title: Group configuration
comparative.config.caption: Pick a categorical column and define which values belong to each group. The groups need not be vegetation — any meaningful dimension (crop, management, season, farm) can be used.
comparative.config.pattern_help: Values whose label contains the pattern go to Group A; the rest go to Group B. E.g.: "mata", "no-till", "control", "rainy".
comparative.config.pattern_no_match: No level contains that pattern; classify the groups manually.
comparative.config.few_levels: Selected column does not contain at least two distinct levels.
comparative.config.overlap: Values [{values}] belong to both groups. Remove the duplicate.
comparative.config.empty_groups: Select at least one value in each group.
comparative.config.too_few_per_group: Each group needs at least 3 records for the comparative analysis.
comparative.summary.title: Mean ± SE summary per group
comparative.summary.caption: Mean, standard error and median of the selected variables. Includes Mann-Whitney U test for pairwise significance.
comparative.summary.missing_scipy: scipy not available for the Mann-Whitney test.
comparative.loglinear.title: Log-linear regression
comparative.loglinear.caption: Only records with the dependent variable strictly positive are used (natural log). An independent model is fitted per group.
comparative.loglinear.too_few_numeric: At least two numeric variables are required.
comparative.hourly.title: Hourly pattern and cumulative flux
comparative.hourly.caption: Aggregation by hour of day: mean of the chosen variable and cumulative hourly sum per group.
comparative.hourly.no_data: No valid records for the hourly analysis.
sidebar.rep.mediana_note: With n=2 replicates (Chl a, Chl b), the median is mathematically identical to the mean. The robustness gain only applies to IAF (n=3), where the median drops the most extreme of the three readings.
pipeline.info_intro: This pipeline runs the following structured steps:
pipeline.info_step_1: **Standardization:** Trimming of extra whitespace in text columns.
pipeline.info_step_2: **Metadata filter:** Removal of rows lacking essential fields (Cultura, Uso atual, Época).
pipeline.info_step_3: **Empty-grid filter:** Removal of sampling-grid rows with no actual measurements.
pipeline.info_step_4: **Replicate handling:** Consolidation or unfolding of Chlorophyll and LAI according to the selected mode.
filters.title: Settings and Filters Panel
filters.description:
Use the filters below to restrict the analysis to specific crops, locations, or time periods.
Changes will be applied reactively across all charts and models.
exp.title: Experimental Statistics
exp.intro: Analyze CRD, RCBD or factorial experiments: ANOVA, assumptions and mean comparison (Tukey/Scott-Knott). Works with any dataset — map the columns below.
exp.warn_no_data: Load a dataset on the Upload page to use experimental statistics.
exp.no_numeric: No numeric column found to use as the response variable.
exp.no_cat: No categorical column found to use as a treatment.
exp.config.title: Column mapping
exp.config.caption: Set the role of each column. The design is detected automatically.
exp.config.block_help: Set a block for RCBD analysis (local control of gradients).
exp.config.factor2_help: Add a second factor for factorial analysis with interaction.
exp.config.block_factor_clash: Block and 2nd factor cannot be the same column.
exp.config.few_levels: The treatment needs at least 2 levels after dropping missing values.
exp.config.few_rows: Too few valid observations for the ANOVA (minimum of 4).
exp.anova.title: Analysis of Variance table
exp.assumptions.title: Assumption tests
exp.assumptions.caption: ANOVA assumes normal residuals and homogeneous variances. p > 0.05 means the assumption holds.
exp.assumptions.violated: At least one assumption was violated (p ≤ 0.05). Consider transforming the data or using non-parametric tests.
exp.compare.title: Mean comparison
exp.compare.caption: Means followed by the same letter do not differ by the chosen test (5%).
exp.compare.no_data: Not enough data for the comparison.
exp.compare.legend: Error bars = standard error of the mean. Letters = statistical grouping.
exp.code.title: Reproducible script
exp.code.caption: Download the Python code that reproduces this analysis to audit, repeat or extend it elsewhere.
exp.mode.label: Analysis type
exp.config.latin_help: Set row and column for a Latin square design — controls two orthogonal gradients. Overrides block/2nd factor.
exp.config.row_col_clash: Row and column cannot be the same column.
exp.dose.title: Dose regression
exp.dose.caption: Polynomial fit of the response to a quantitative factor (fertilizer dose, irrigation depth, density…).
exp.dose.need_numeric: At least two numeric columns are required (dose and response).
exp.dose.top_term_ns: The highest-order term was not significant (p ≥ 0.05); consider lowering the polynomial degree.
exp.corr.title: Correlation
exp.corr.caption: Correlation matrix between numeric variables, with optional partial correlation.
exp.corr.partial_help: Correlation between two variables after removing the effect of one or more control covariates.
exp.corr.partial_pick_covar: Choose at least one control covariate.
exp.config.numeric_as_factor_help: Numeric columns that encode factors (block, replicate, row…). Selected ones become factors and leave the response list.
exp.config.no_response_left: No numeric column left as the response variable. Deselect one in 'Treat as factor'.
exp.config.factor3_help: Add a third factor for a three-way factorial analysis (all interactions). Requires the 2nd factor.
exp.config.covariate_help: Continuous numeric variable to adjust the response (e.g., initial weight, litter size). Means become adjusted (marginal means).
exp.anova.covariate_info: Covariate **{cov}**: slope = {slope} (p = {p}). Treatment means below are adjusted for it.
exp.compare.adjusted_note: Means adjusted for covariate {cov} (ANCOVA), compared using the fitted model's error.
exp.config.covariate_clash: The covariate cannot also be used as a factor/block.
